description: Staging area for CANDIDATE version sets awaiting canary certification. `scripts/canary.mjs` applies one candidate to a fresh full-featured scaffold, builds it for real (Android assembleDebug + the device-free lane gates + the iOS framework link), and ONLY on all-green promotes it into registry.json as a new proven-green set (the new default `upgrade` target). Red leaves registry.json untouched. Same shape as a registry set, plus `status: candidate` and `baseline` (the proven-green set it derives from). Keys under `versions` mirror the [versions] table of the golden template's gradle/libs.versions.toml — store the FULL resolved set so a promoted entry is a complete snapshot.

candidates.label: Bold — Kotlin 2.3.10 + KSP 2.3.10 + Compose 1.11.1. BLOCKED (canary 2026-07-15): KSP 2.3.10 needs a newer AGP than 8.7.3 (see notes). Next attempt must bump AGP (and likely Gradle) with it.
notes: Bold compiler jump vs 2026.07c: kotlin 2.2.20→2.3.10, ksp 2.2.20-2.0.4→2.3.10 (KSP2 dropped the -<ksp> suffix; ksp now equals the Kotlin version), compose-multiplatform 1.10.3→1.11.1, sqlite 2.6.2→2.7.0, koin 4.1.1→4.2.2, firebase-gitlive 2.1.0→2.4.0.

Held to isolate risk: AGP 8.7.3 (avoid the AGP-9 major), ktor 3.1.0 (3.2.0 needs DEX 040 / newer R8), gradle wrapper 8.11.1. Kotlin 2.4.10 is available but has NO matching KSP yet (KSP tops out at 2.3.10) — so 2.3.10 is the max Kotlin that can run Room's KSP processor.

CANARY RESULT (2026-07-15, red at config in 34s): KSP 2.3.10 calls com.android.build.api.variant.AndroidComponentsExtension.addKspConfigurations(boolean), which AGP 8.7.3 does not expose → NoSuchMethodError. The Kotlin-2.3.10/KSP-2.3.10 jump is COUPLED to an AGP bump; holding AGP at 8.7.3 is not an option. Next attempt: bump AGP to a version exposing addKspConfigurations (and the Gradle wrapper it requires), then re-run — Compose 1.11.1 ↔ Kotlin 2.3.10 and compose-hot-reload 1.1.1 compatibility are still unverified (the build died before reaching them).
